Name: Bulbasaur
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: While it is young, it uses the nutrients that are stored in the seed on its back in order to grow.

Name: Ivysaur
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Exposure to sunlight adds to its strength. Sunlight also makes the bud on its back grow larger.

Name: Venusaur ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.
Flavor-Text: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Charmeleon
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

Name: Charizard ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.
Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Squirtle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: When it feels threatened, it draws its limbs inside its shell and sprays water from its mouth.

Name: Wartortle
Effects: Put up to 3 Basic [W] Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It cleverly controls its furry ears and tail to maintain its balance while swimming.

Name: Blastoise ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Discard up to 2 Basic [W] Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 140 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Caterpie
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [G] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

Name: Metapod
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can’t withstand a harsh attack.

Name: Butterfree
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

Choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. Shuffle that Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck, and then shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It collects honey every day. It rubs honey onto the hairs on its legs to carry it back to its nest.

Name: Weedle
Flavor-Text: Its poison stinger is very powerful. Its bright-colored body is intended to warn off its enemies.

Name: Kakuna
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Flavor-Text: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

Name: Beedrill
Effects: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 120 more damage, and your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: May appear in a swarm. Flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.

Name: Pidgey
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It is docile and prefers to avoid conflict. If disturbed, however, it can ferociously strike back.

Name: Pidgeotto
Flavor-Text: Very protective of its sprawling territorial area, this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

Name: Pidgeot
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It spreads its gorgeous wings widely to intimidate enemies. It races through the skies at Mach-2 speed.

Name: Rattata
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is common but hazardous. Its sharp incisors can easily cut right through hard wood.

Name: Raticate
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its whiskers are essential for maintaining its balance. No matter how friendly you are, it will get angry and bite you if you touch its whiskers.

Name: Spearow
Ability-Effect: If you go second, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn.
Flavor-Text: Due to its short wings, it can’t fly long distances. It wanders about restlessly and pecks at bug Pokémon.

Name: Fearow
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Carrying food through Fearow’s territory is dangerous. It will snatch the food away from you in a flash!

Name: Ekans
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The eggs of bird Pokémon are its favorite food. It swallows eggs whole, so sometimes an egg gets stuck, and Ekans faints.

Name: Arbok ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Your opponent discards 2 cards from their hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: ﻿When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

Name: Raichu
Ability-Effect: When 1 of your Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, you may move a [L] Energy from that Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

Name: Sandshrew
Ability-Effect: Trainer cards in your opponent’s discard pile can’t be put into their deck by an effect of your opponent’s Item or Supporter cards.
Flavor-Text: It burrows into the ground to create its nest. If hard stones impede its tunneling, it uses its sharp claws to shatter them and then carries on digging.

Name: Sandslash
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It climbs trees by hooking on with its sharp claws. Sandslash shares the berries it gathers, dropping them down to Sandshrew waiting below the tree.

Name: Nidoran Female
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It uses its hard incisor teeth to crush and eat berries. The tip of a female Nidoran’s horn is a bit more rounded than the tip of a male’s horn.

Name: Nidorina
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: If the group is threatened, these Pokémon will band together to assault enemies with a chorus of ultrasonic waves.

Name: Nidoqueen
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It pacifies offspring by placing them in the gaps between the spines on its back. The spines will never secrete poison while young are present.

Name: Nidoran Male
Flavor-Text: Small but brave, this Pokémon will hold its ground and even risk its life in battle to protect the female it’s friendly with.

Name: Nidorino
Flavor-Text: It’s nervous and quick to act aggressively. The potency of its poison increases along with the level of adrenaline present in its body.

Name: Nidoking
Ability-Effect: If you have Nidoqueen in play, ignore all Energy in the costs of attacks used by this Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Nidoking prides itself on its strength. It’s forceful and spirited in battle, making use of its thick tail and diamond-crushing horn.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Clefairy and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its adorable behavior and cry make it highly popular. However, this cute Pokémon is rarely found.

Name: Clefable
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.
Flavor-Text: Their ears are sensitive enough to hear a pin drop from over a mile away, so they’re usually found in quiet places.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

Name: Ninetales ex
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 140 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When its huge eyes waver, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

Name: Wigglytuff ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, it gets +100 HP.
Effects: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zubat
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may have your opponent reveal their hand.
Flavor-Text: Zubat live in caves, down where the sun’s light won’t reach. In the morning, they gather together to keep each other warm as they sleep.

Name: Golbat
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its feet are tiny, but this Pokémon walks skillfully. It sneaks up on sleeping prey before sinking in its fangs and slurping up blood.

Name: Oddish
Flavor-Text: During the day, it stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.

Name: Gloom
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and attach any number of Basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: What appears to be drool is actually sweet honey. It is very sticky and clings stubbornly if touched.

Name: Vileplume
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may look at the top 8 cards of your deck and attach any number of Basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: The larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.

Name: Paras
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.

Name: Parasect
Effects: Flip 2 coins. Search your deck for a number of [G] Pokémon up to the number of heads and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.

Name: Venonat
Flavor-Text: Poison oozes from all over its body. It catches small bug Pokémon at night that are attracted by light.

Name: Venomoth
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Item cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: The wings are covered with dustlike scales. Every time it flaps its wings, it looses highly toxic dust.

Name: Diglett
Flavor-Text: It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

Name: Dugtrio
Flavor-Text: Its three heads bob separately up and down to loosen the soil nearby, making it easier for it to burrow.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

Name: Persian
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Giovanni’s Charisma card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.

Name: Psyduck
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, whenever they flip a coin, treat it as tails.
Flavor-Text: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

Name: Golduck
Effects: Put up to 4 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: When it swims at full speed using its long, webbed limbs, its forehead somehow begins to glow.

Name: Mankey
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

Name: Primeape
Effects: This Pokémon is now Confused.
If this Pokémon isn’t Confused, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Discard a [W] Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

Name: Arcanine
Effects: Attach up to 2 Basic [R] Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.

Name: Poliwag
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: In rivers with fast-flowing water, this Pokémon will cling to a rock by using its thick lips, which act like a suction cup.

Name: Poliwhirl
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s sweat is a slimy mucus. When captured, Poliwhirl can slither from its enemies’ grasp and escape.

Name: Poliwrath
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 150 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Poliwrath is skilled at both swimming and martial arts. It uses its well-trained arms to dish out powerful punches.

Name: Abra
Flavor-Text: Abra can teleport in its sleep. Apparently the more deeply Abra sleeps, the farther its teleportations go.

Name: Kadabra
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s telekinesis is immensely powerful. To prepare for evolution, Kadabra stores up psychic energy in the star on its forehead.

Name: Alakazam ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
This attack can be used even if this Pokémon is on the Bench.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Machop
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Always brimming with power, it passes time by lifting boulders. Doing so makes it even stronger.

Name: Machoke
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Its formidable body never gets tired. It helps people by doing work such as the moving of heavy goods.

Name: Machamp
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: With four arms that react more quickly than it can think, it can execute many punches at once.

Name: Bellsprout
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Its bud looks like a human face. Because of the bud, it is rumored to be a type of legendary mandrake plant.

Name: Weepinbell
Flavor-Text: It spits out Poison Powder to immobilize the enemy and then finishes it with a spray of Acid.

Name: Victreebel
Effects: At the end of your opponent’s next turn, put 12 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Once ingested into this Pokémon’s body, even the hardest object will melt into nothing.

Name: Tentacool
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is mostly made of water. A Tentacool out in the ocean is very hard to spot, because its body blends in with the sea.

Name: Tentacruel
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 90 damage for each heads. If the first flip is tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its 80 tentacles can stretch and shrink freely. Tentacruel ensnares prey in a net of spread-out tentacles, delivering venomous stings to its catch.

Name: Geodude
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It uses both hands to climb precipitous cliffs. People who see it in action have been known to take up bouldering.

Name: Graveler
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It travels by rolling down cliffs. If it falls into a river, it will explode with its last gasp.

Name: Golem ex
Effects: During your next turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ponyta
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: If you’ve been accepted by Ponyta, its burning mane is mysteriously no longer hot to the touch.

Name: Rapidash
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The fastest runner becomes the leader, and it decides the herd’s pace and direction of travel.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.
Flavor-Text: It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

Name: Slowbro
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.
If this Pokémon evolved during this turn, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When a Slowpoke went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a Shellder. That made it evolve into Slowbro.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

Name: Magneton
Effects: Put up to 2 Item cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

Name: Farfetch’d
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: They use a plant stalk as a weapon, but not all of them use it in the same way. Several distinct styles of stalk fighting have been observed.

Name: Doduo
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

Name: Dodrio
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 1 damage counter on this Pokémon. If you do, draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An odd species that is rarely found. The three heads respectively represent joy, sadness, and anger.

Name: Seel
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its thick fat, cold seas don’t bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.

Name: Dewgong
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It sunbathes on the beach after meals. The rise in its body temperature helps its digestion.

Name: Grimer
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C] more.
Flavor-Text: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.

Name: Muk
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost [C] more, and its Retreat Cost is [C] more.
Flavor-Text: It’s thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

Name: Shellder
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It is encased in a shell that is harder than diamond. Inside, however, it is surprisingly tender.

Name: Cloyster
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 80 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Cloyster that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.

Name: Gastly
Flavor-Text: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

Name: Haunter
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put a Supporter card from your opponent’s discard pile into their hand.
Flavor-Text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

Name: Gengar
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.
Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: To steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.

Name: Onix
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 80 damage for each Pokémon with a Retreat Cost of exactly 4 that you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

Name: Drowzee
Flavor-Text: It remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children’s are much tastier.

Name: Hypno
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of psi moves, such as Hypnosis and Confusion.

Name: Krabby
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 2 Basic [W] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: If it senses danger approaching, it cloaks itself with bubbles from its mouth so it will look bigger.

Name: Kingler
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Its oversized claw is very powerful, but when it’s not in battle, the claw just gets in the way.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

Name: Electrode
Effects: Before doing damage, you may discard any number of Pokémon Tools from your Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon get nervous when they’re not in a group of six. The minute even one member of the group goes missing, Exeggcute become cowardly.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When they work together, Exeggutor’s three heads can put out powerful psychic energy. Cloudy days make this Pokémon sluggish.

Name: Cubone
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, attacks used by your Marowak do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon wears the skull of its deceased mother. Sometimes Cubone’s dreams make it cry, but each tear Cubone sheds makes it stronger.

Name: Marowak
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: When this Pokémon evolved, the skull of its mother fused to it. Marowak’s temperament also turned vicious at the same time.

Name: Hitmonlee
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The legs freely contract and stretch. The stretchy legs allow it to hit a distant foe with a rising kick.

Name: Hitmonchan
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Excited Punch attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.

Name: Lickitung
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Bug Pokémon are Lickitung’s main food source. This Pokémon paralyzes its prey with a lick from its long tongue, then swallows the prey whole.

Name: Koffing
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

Name: Weezing
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, the Attacking Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Very rarely, a sudden mutation can result in two small Koffing twins becoming conjoined as a Weezing.

Name: Rhyhorn
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It can remember only one thing at a time. Once it starts rushing, it forgets why it started.

Name: Rhydon
Effects: If you played Giovanni’s Charisma from your hand during this turn, this attack does 140 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Chansey
Ability-Effect: If you took this Pokémon as a face-down Prize card during your turn and your Bench isn’t full, before you put it into your hand, you may put it onto your Bench. If you put this Pokémon onto your Bench in this way, flip a coin. If heads, take 1 more Prize card.
Flavor-Text: This kindly Pokémon lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

Name: Tangela
Effects: If you played Erika’s Invitation from your hand during this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

Name: Kangaskhan ex
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Horsea
Flavor-Text: They swim with dance-like motions and cause whirlpools to form. Horsea compete to see which of them can generate the biggest whirlpool.

Name: Seadra
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips 2 coins. If either of them is tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
Flavor-Text: Seadra’s mouth is slender, but its suction power is strong. In an instant, Seadra can suck in food that’s larger than the opening of its mouth.

Name: Goldeen
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its dorsal and pectoral fins are strongly developed like muscles. It can swim at a speed of five knots.

Name: Seaking
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
This attack does 30 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using its horn, it bores holes in riverbed boulders, making nests to prevent its eggs from washing away.

Name: Staryu
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Fish Pokémon nibble at it, but Staryu isn’t bothered. Its body regenerates quickly, even if part of it is completely torn off.

Name: Starmie
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If you placed any damage counters in this way, discard this Pokémon and all attached cards.
Flavor-Text: Starmie swims by spinning its body at high speed. As this Pokémon cruises through the ocean, it absorbs tiny plankton.

Name: Mr. Mime
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon and your opponent’s Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It’s known for its top-notch pantomime skills. It protects itself from all sorts of attacks by emitting auras from its fingers to create walls.

Name: Scyther
Effects: Attach a Basic [G] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

Name: Jynx ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Asleep, it is Knocked Out.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: If Magmar is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: With the coming of a storm, many of these Pokémon will gather under tall trees and sit there waiting for lightning to strike.

Name: Magmar
Effects: If Electabuzz is on your Bench, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon’s bodies are constantly burning. Magmar are feared as one of the causes behind fires.

Name: Pinsir
Effects: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon clamps its pincers down on its prey and then either splits the prey in half or flings it away.

Name: Tauros
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, draw a card.
Flavor-Text: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

Name: Gyarados
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you must discard the top 5 cards of your deck.
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.

Name: Ditto
Ability-Effect: Once during your first turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may search your deck and choose a Basic Pokémon you find there, except any Ditto. If you do, discard this Pokémon and all attached cards, and put the chosen Pokémon in its place. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its transformation ability is perfect. However, if made to laugh, it can’t maintain its disguise.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon of different types, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

Name: Jolteon
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

Name: Flareon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Porygon
Effects: Choose [G], [R], [W], [L], [P], [F], [D], [M], or [N] type. Until the Defending Pokémon leaves the Active Spot, its Weakness is now that type. (The amount of Weakness doesn’t change.)
Flavor-Text: In recent years, this species has been very helpful in cyberspace. These Pokémon will go around checking to make sure no suspicious data exists.

Name: Omanyte
Effects: Put an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon into their hand.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a member of an ancient, extinct species. Omanyte paddles through water with its 10 tentacles, looking like it’s just drifting along.

Name: Omastar
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Active Pokémon can’t retreat.
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Omastar’s sharp fangs could crush rock, but the Pokémon can attack only the prey that come within reach of its tentacles.

Name: Kabuto
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: While some say this species has gone extinct, Kabuto sightings are apparently fairly common in some places.

Name: Kabutops
Ability-Effect: Apply Weakness for your opponent’s Active Pokémon as ×4 instead.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The cause behind the extinction of this species is unknown. Kabutops were aggressive Pokémon that inhabited warm seas.

Name: Aerodactyl
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an evolved Pokémon, devolve it by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Aerodactyl’s sawlike fangs can shred skin to tatters—even the skin of Steel-type Pokémon.

Name: Snorlax
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put up to 2 Leftovers cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.

Name: Articuno
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can control ice at will. Articuno is said to live in snowy mountains riddled with permafrost.

Name: Zapdos ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [L] Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: This attack also does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Moltres
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [R] Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: There are stories of this Pokémon using its radiant, flame-cloaked wings to light up paths for those lost in the mountains.

Name: Dratini
Flavor-Text: It sheds many layers of skin as it grows larger. During this process, it is protected by a rapid waterfall.

Name: Dragonair
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: They say that if it emits an aura from its whole body, the weather will begin to change instantly.

Name: Dragonite
Ability-Effect: Your Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: It is said that somewhere in the ocean lies an island where these gather. Only they live there.

Name: Mewtwo
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew’s genes. It’s said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

Name: Mew ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Antique Dome Fossil
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Description: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic [C] Pokémon. This card can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Antique Helix Fossil
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can’t play any Stadium cards from their hand.
Description: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic [C] Pokémon. This card can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Antique Old Amber
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to this Pokémon.
Description: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic [C] Pokémon. This card can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Big Air Balloon
Description: The Stage 2 Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Bill’s Transfer
Description: Look at the top 8 cards of your deck. You may reveal any number of Pokémon you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Cycling Road
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may discard a Basic Energy card from their hand in order to draw a card.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Daisy’s Help
Description: Draw 2 cards. Look at your face-down Prize cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Energy Sticker
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Erika’s Invitation
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you put a Basic Pokémon you find there onto your opponent’s Bench. If you put a Pokémon onto their Bench in this way, switch in that Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Giovanni’s Charisma
Description: Put an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon into their hand. If you do, attach an Energy card from your hand to your Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Grabber
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you put a Pokémon you find there on the bottom of their deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Leftovers
Description: At the end of your turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, heal 20 damage from it.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Protective Goggles
Description: The Basic Pokémon this card is attached to has no Weakness.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Rigid Band
Description: The Stage 1 Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Bulbasaur
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: While it is young, it uses the nutrients that are stored in the seed on its back in order to grow.

Name: Ivysaur
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Exposure to sunlight adds to its strength. Sunlight also makes the bud on its back grow larger.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.
Flavor-Text: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Charmeleon
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

Name: Squirtle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: When it feels threatened, it draws its limbs inside its shell and sprays water from its mouth.

Name: Wartortle
Effects: Put up to 3 Basic [W] Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It cleverly controls its furry ears and tail to maintain its balance while swimming.

Name: Caterpie
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [G] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: ﻿When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

Name: Nidoking
Ability-Effect: If you have Nidoqueen in play, ignore all Energy in the costs of attacks used by this Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Nidoking prides itself on its strength. It’s forceful and spirited in battle, making use of its thick tail and diamond-crushing horn.

Name: Psyduck
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, whenever they flip a coin, treat it as tails.
Flavor-Text: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

Name: Poliwhirl
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s sweat is a slimy mucus. When captured, Poliwhirl can slither from its enemies’ grasp and escape.

Name: Machoke
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Its formidable body never gets tired. It helps people by doing work such as the moving of heavy goods.

Name: Tangela
Effects: If you played Erika’s Invitation from your hand during this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

Name: Mr. Mime
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon and your opponent’s Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It’s known for its top-notch pantomime skills. It protects itself from all sorts of attacks by emitting auras from its fingers to create walls.

Effects: Put an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon into their hand.

Name: Dragonair
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.

Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
